content.full_text:
Fern Polrucar PnecrrcEs CouvnssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
Apil22,2004
Judy Hauff, Deputy City Clerk
City of Rohnert Park
Administrative Offices
6750 Commerce Boulevard
Rohnert Park, CA 94928
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-04-084
Dear Ms. Hauff:
This letter is in response to your request on behalf of the City of Rohnert Park
for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the '?.ct").1
QUESTION
May candidates and committees involved in a recall election to be held in August
2004 corttbine the first pre-election campaign statanent with the first semi-annual
statement due July 31,2004?
CONCLUSION
In connection with an August 2004 special election, candidates and committees
may use a filing schedule that combines the pre-election campaign statements with the
semi-annual statement due on July 31, 2004.
FACTS
The Rohnert Park City Council will be accepting the city clerk's certification of
results at an upcoming meeting after the examination of signatures on recall petitions for
two city council members. The numbers are sufficient to require special elections for
both and the announcennent of the city council's scheduling of a special election for these
recalls will be made at its next meeting on April 27,2004. The nominations open
immediately upon the call of the election.
I Government Code sections 81000 - gl}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. 4-04-084
Page No. 2
You are in the process of preparing nomination papers for candidates, which
include providing the usual nomination process calendax of listed deadlines including
campaign statements. Regardless of which Tuesday in August 2004the city council may
choose to call the special election for these two recalls, you request a schedule combining
the semi-annual and pre-election campaign statement requirements of the filing schedule
for an August special election.
ANALYSIS
During an election, candidates and committees involved in the election are
required to file two campaign statements disclosing contibutions received and
expenditures made in connection with that election. These pre-election campaign
statements are generally filed 40 days before the election and again 12 daysbefore the
election. (Section 84200.8.) Semi-annual statements are filed January 31 and July 31,
respectively. (Section 84200.)
There is a mechanism in the Act that permits the combining of statements when
an overlapping reporting period occurs, or the filing of the semi-annual stateme,lrt would
be an additional, unnecessary filing to the two pre-election statements. (Section 84205.)
In this case, filing the two pre-election staternents in connection with an August election
will satisft the semi-annual filing requirement.
You state that it is likely the election will be held on August 24. A campaign
statement filing schedule which outlines the due dates for filing statements in connection
with an August 24 electionis enclosed for your convenience. If the election is scheduled
for a different date in August, we will be happy to provide you with a revised filing
schedule.
If I may be of any further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me at (866)
275-3772.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
Cv,',1,*OJ^d1*-^)
By: Cmla Wardlow
Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division
Enclosure

CONCLUSION: In connection with an August 2004 special election, candidates and committees
